evidences.title: Binance Historical Data API: A Developer's Guide
evidences.content: Parameters: + `symbol` (required): The trading pair (e.g., `BTCUSDT`). + `interval` (required): The candlestick interval (e.g., `1m`, `5m`, `1h`, `1d`). + `startTime` (optional): The timestamp (in milliseconds) for the start of the data. + `endTime` (optional): The timestamp (in milliseconds) for the end of the data. + `limit` (optional): The number of records to return (max 1000). Example Request (Python): ``` import requests url = ' params = { 'symbol': 'BTCUSDT', 'interval': '1d', 'startTime': 1609459200000, # January 1, 2021 'limit': 1000 } response = requests.get(url, params=params) data = response.json() print(data) ``` [...] # Raise an exception for HTTP errors return response.json() # Example usage symbol = 'BTCUSDT' interval = '1h' start_time = 1609459200000 # January 1, 2021 data = get_historical_data(symbol, interval, start_time) print(data) [...] Understanding and utilizing the key endpoints for historical data, particularly the `/api/v3/klines` endpoint, is essential for anyone working with the Binance historical price data API. The `/api/v3/klines` endpoint provides a wealth of information in the form of OHLCV data, allowing you to analyze price movements and tradi...

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation: Introduction
evidences.content: + Previously some requests failed if the `startTime` == `endTime`. + Now, all API requests that accept `startTime` and `endTime` allow the parameters to be equal. This applies to the following requests: - `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` - `GET /api/v3/klines` - `GET /api/v3/allOrderList` - `GET /api/v3/allOrders` - `GET /api/v3/myTrades` Changes to Filter Evaluation: + Previous behavior: `LOT_SIZE` and `MARKET_LOT_SIZE` required that (`quantity` - `minQty`) % `stepSize` == 0. + New behavior changed to: (`quantity` % `stepSize`) == 0. Bug fix with reverse `MARKET` orders (i.e. `MARKET` using `quoteOrderQty`): [...] > Response ``` [{" a ": 874844, " p ": "0.00379700 ", " q ": "0.05000000 ", " f ": 981493, " l ": 981493, " T ": 1637128220041, " m ": true, " M ": true}] ``` `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` Use this endpoint to get compressed, aggregate trades. Trades that fill at the same time, from the same order, with the same price, will have the quantity aggregated. Please note the maximum limit is 1,000 trades. Weight: 1 Parameters: | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | | fromId | LONG | NO | ID to get aggregate trades from INCLUSIVE | | startTime | LONG...

evidences.title: THE NATURE OF THE 1-MINUTE CHART
evidences.content: Article # THE NATURE OF THE 1-MINUTE CHART Daily Clever News Nov 11, 2025 Follow View Hidden Replies Report Block User Analyzing the 1-minute chart (1m chart) is an essential skill for scalpers — traders who operate on short-term moves, entering and exiting positions within a few minutes to a few tens of minutes. To master this timeframe, you need to understand its nature, use the right tools, and maintain absolute discipline in every decision. $BTC $BNB $ETH UNDERSTANDING THE NATURE OF THE 1M CHART The 1-minute chart reflects the smallest and fastest price movements. Each candle represents one minute of trading, so signals appear constantly and are highly noisy. This timeframe is best used to find precise entry and exit points, not to predict long-term trends. [...] CONCLUSION The 1-minute chart is not for predicting the future — it’s for observing how price reacts in real time. It demands focus, reflexes, and iron discipline. Once you understand the rhythm of each candle and the flow of capital within this tiny window, you’ll stop chasing the market — and start moving in sync with it. Disclaimer: Includes third-party opinions. No financial advice. May include sponsored content...
